levelLabel: L-01 pre-programming -- the smallest possible program

steps.annotation: a .cpp file is text the compiler reads. The first line tells the compiler to also pull in cin/cout.
every program enters at main(). The OS literally calls this function.
the compiler turned this line into machine instructions; running them prints to your terminal.
control returns to the OS with status 0 (success).

levelLabel: L0 source skeleton -- hello world end-to-end

steps.annotation: pulls cin/cout/endl into scope.
lets you write cout instead of std::cout (SIT102 convention).
entry point. Returns int (0 = success).
<< chains to cout. ; terminates.
reports success.
closes main's body.

levelLabel: L1 output -- chained values + printf for Q4

steps.annotation: declare an int, store 42.
declare a string, store the literal.
cout chain: int + space + string + newline.
Q4 printf shape. %d for int, %s for c_str, \n for newline.

levelLabel: L10 arrays -- declare, fill, walk, sum

steps.annotation: declare and initialize a fixed-size array.
walk 0..4. arr[5] would be out of bounds.
arr[i] reads the i-th slot.

levelLabel: L11 structs -- define, declare, write, read

steps.annotation: open the type definition.
each field: type fieldName;
; required after } when defining a type.
now book is a usable type.
. accesses one field; = writes it.
reading fields via .field

levelLabel: L12 pass composites -- struct and array via &

steps.annotation: & on struct param: caller and d are the SAME struct.
writing d.mystery persists in caller (PC-03).
pass d by ref; no special syntax at call site.
main sees the mutation: prints 7.
remember & means main's d and data are the same struct.
overwrite data.mystery to 0; record this state.
pick branch by current values of data.a and data.b.
if true, write data.a into data.mystery.
else, write data.b. only ONE side runs.
header: keyword + name + opening brace.
pick int for ids.
string for text fields.
double for amounts.
closing brace + ; -- skipping ; fails the grader.
void return + array-by-ref + int count = Q3 signature shape.
walk 0..count-1.
prompt then cin into one field.
one cin per field; order matches struct definition.
Q4 always asks for a complete main.
capacity constant; declared FIRST.
array of structs sized to MAX.
storage for actual record count.
user types record count.
call Q3 to fill the array.
print loop walks the records.
printf with %d, %s, %.2f, .c_str() for the string.
main ends with success.

levelLabel: L17 mock exam -- the four shapes back-to-back

steps.annotation: predict d.mystery after who_am_i runs (HE-* atoms).
three fields, types from spec, ; after } (SW-* atoms).
void + &list[] + int count + for-loop + cin per field (RW-* atoms).
const MAX + list[MAX] + count + cin + call read + printf loop (MW-* atoms).

levelLabel: L2 variables -- declare, init, mutate, read

steps.annotation: declare an int box named n. Value undefined until written.
assignment writes 5 into the box.
declare + initialize in one line; double for decimals.
string holds text; needs <string> include.
reading variables means using their names in expressions.
declare destination box.
always prompt before cin so the user knows what to type.
cin pauses, reads the typed token, parses to int, stores in n.
echo back so the user can confirm.

levelLabel: L4 operators -- arithmetic with order of operations

steps.annotation: declare and store.
* binds tighter than +; here width * height = 12.
() forces + first: 2 * (4 + 3) = 14.

levelLabel: L5 comparison + logical -- bool from compare

steps.annotation: >= returns bool; stored directly.
> compares greater-than.
|| chains two bools; true if either is true.

levelLabel: L6 conditionals -- if / else if / else

steps.annotation: first test; runs body if true.
else if chains another test only when prior was false.
else runs when no prior test matched.
init runs once, cond before each, step after.
body runs while cond is true.
signature: returns int, named square, takes one int param.
compute and hand value back to the caller.
caller binds 5 to n inside the function frame, gets 25 back.
& between type and name makes x an alias for caller box.
mutation writes through the alias to caller.
caller variable.
no & at call site; binding happens inside the function frame.
prints 6 (mutated), not 5. Compare to R-01/R-02 (no &).
